summary: Re-verified deploy/packaging fixes for `craco: command not found` and the iteration-2 residual regression (`Cannot find module 'dotenv'` under NODE_ENV=production). All three fixes are correctly applied and both build scenarios (default + NODE_ENV=production) now succeed end-to-end with GitHub-Pages-safe relative asset paths.

verification_results.package.json_deps_check: PASS — @craco/craco, gh-pages, dotenv all in dependencies; none in devDependencies
verification_results.craco_config_trycatch: PASS — require('dotenv').config() is wrapped in try/catch in /app/frontend/craco.config.js
verification_results.fresh_install_default: PASS — jba1: yarn install --frozen-lockfile + CI=false yarn build succeeded; build/index.html has src="./static/js/main.5598647d.js" (relative)
verification_results.fresh_install_NODE_ENV_production: PASS — jba2: NODE_ENV=production yarn install --frozen-lockfile + CI=false yarn build succeeded; same relative asset paths. The iteration-2 failure case is now fixed.
verification_results.supervisor_status: PASS — backend, frontend, mongodb, code-server all RUNNING

context_for_next_testing_agent: Packaging fix fully verified. yarn deploy flow works from a fresh clone in both default and NODE_ENV=production modes.
rca of the issue: Previously fixed. Root cause was build-time deps (@craco/craco, gh-pages, dotenv) living in devDependencies and being skipped under NODE_ENV=production. Now all three are in dependencies AND dotenv require is defensively try/catch-wrapped in craco.config.js.
